notable_pick_trades: Traded 1st-round pick (No. 29), 5th-round pick, 6th-round pick in 2026 and 2027 3rd-round pick to KC for CB Trent McDuffie. Own No. 13 overall via 2025 draft-day trade with Atlanta.
roster_context: NFC Championship team with reigning MVP Matthew Stafford (age 38, year-to-year commitment). Elite offense ranked 1st in DVOA in 2025, but secondary was the clear Achilles heel (19th in passing defense). Traded four picks for All-Pro CB Trent McDuffie to address biggest weakness. Rob Havenstein retired, creating RT question. Win-now mode with Stafford's window closing.

needs.explanation: Even after trading for Trent McDuffie, CB remains a top need. The Rams ranked 19th in passing defense and lost in the NFC Championship largely due to secondary play. Cobie Durant, Ahkello Witherspoon, Roger McCreary, and Derion Kendrick are all pending free agents. McDuffie fills one spot, but the CB2/nickel role and depth are barren. Darious Williams is a potential cut candidate.
needs.current_starter: Trent McDuffie (acquired via trade), Darious Williams / TBD
needs.depth: Extremely thin — four CBs hitting free agency. Only McDuffie is locked in.

needs.explanation: Rob Havenstein retired after 11 seasons, leaving a hole at RT. Warren McClendon Jr. stepped in for 13 starts in 2025 and graded well (8th among tackles per PFF), and the Rams are looking to extend him. However, McClendon is on the final year of his rookie deal and RT still needs insurance/competition. Protecting 38-year-old MVP Stafford is paramount.
needs.depth: D.J. Humphries and David Quessenberry are UFAs. McClendon moving to starter leaves no swing tackle depth.

needs.explanation: Safety Kamren Curl is a UFA after a breakout 2025 (122 tackles, 2 INTs, 17 starts). The Rams re-signed Quentin Lake to a 3-year, $42M extension, but Lake plays a hybrid safety/nickel role. If Curl leaves in free agency, the Rams need a traditional strong safety to pair with Lake. The Rams historically don't pay safeties big money.
needs.depth: Very thin if Curl departs. Lake is versatile but can't play every secondary role.

needs.explanation: Puka Nacua (WR1 in 2025) and Davante Adams (age 33, entering final contract year) are elite, but no true WR3 emerged in 2025. Tutu Atwell is a departing UFA after barely playing. Jordan Whittington and rookie Konata Mumpfield showed flashes but aren't proven. Adams' age makes finding a long-term WR2/successor important for sustainability.
needs.depth: Jordan Whittington, Konata Mumpfield — unproven. Tutu Atwell departing.

needs.explanation: Stafford is the reigning MVP but turns 38 and is on a year-to-year commitment. GM Les Snead acknowledged Stafford is 'closer to retirement than not' but said it's 'not a desperate matter.' Backup Jimmy Garoppolo is strongly linked to Arizona as a free agent, leaving only Stetson Bennett behind Stafford. The Rams need a developmental successor, not an immediate starter.
needs.depth: Jimmy Garoppolo (UFA, likely leaving), Stetson Bennett (not viewed as viable backup long-term)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if a QB the staff loves falls, otherwise Round 3+

needs.explanation: The Rams' 13-personnel package was a huge part of their 2025 success with Colby Parkinson (career highs in receptions, yards, TDs), Terrance Ferguson, and Davis Allen. However, Tyler Higbee is a UFA and all three current TEs hit free agency after 2026. Drafting a TE now shores up the position long-term.
needs.current_starter: Colby Parkinson, Terrance Ferguson, Davis Allen
needs.depth: Adequate for 2026 but all three TEs are 2027 free agents. Tyler Higbee (UFA) likely not returning.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 (if Kenyon Sadiq is available at 13, strong consideration)

needs.explanation: Kobie Turner and Braden Fiske are solid starters on the interior, but Turner is a 2027 free agent and the Rams may lean DT for depth. Adding a rotational nose tackle or 3-tech to keep the front line fresh would complement the elite EDGE duo of Jared Verse and Byron Young.
needs.depth: Adequate but could use rotational depth. Turner extension-eligible this offseason.

needs.explanation: The entire interior OL — G Kevin Dotson, C Coleman Shelton, G Steve Avila — hits free agency after 2026. While the unit is intact for now and performed well in 2025 (Stafford sacked only 23 times), investing in IOL depth now would be forward-thinking. Justin Dedich is an ERFA who can be retained cheaply.
needs.current_starter: Steve Avila (LG), Coleman Shelton (C), Kevin Dotson (RG)
needs.depth: Justin Dedich (ERFA). All three starters are 2027 FAs — a looming concern.

free_agency_impact: Traded 1st (No. 29), 5th, 6th-round picks and 2027 3rd to KC for CB Trent McDuffie — significantly addresses CB1 need but reduces draft capital
RT Rob Havenstein retired after 11 seasons — creates RT need, McClendon expected to step up
Re-signed S Quentin Lake to 3-year, $42M extension — FS position locked down
Re-signed LB Nate Landman to 3-year, $22M extension — LB not a pressing need
QB Jimmy Garoppolo strongly linked to Cardinals as UFA — backup QB need increased
WR Tutu Atwell expected to depart as UFA — minimal impact, was not contributing
S Kamren Curl UFA — if he departs, creates significant SS need
CB Cobie Durant, Roger McCreary, Ahkello Witherspoon, Derion Kendrick all UFAs — CB depth devastated even with McDuffie acquisition
TE Tyler Higbee UFA — veteran TE depth reduced but Parkinson/Ferguson/Allen remain
Looking to extend Warren McClendon Jr. (RT), Puka Nacua, Kobie Turner, Byron Young from 2023 draft class
scheme_fit_notes: Sean McVay's offense is built on play-action, outside zone runs, and 11/12/13 personnel packages. The Rams' innovative use of 13-personnel (3 TE sets) was a 2025 breakout — TE prospects with receiving/blocking versatility have elevated value. On defense under Chris Shula, the scheme is front-four driven with Jared Verse and Byron Young as premium EDGE rushers. The secondary needs press-man corners who can play outside and in the slot. Versatile DBs who can match up in both man and zone coverage fit best. IDL prospects who can penetrate as 3-techs complement the edge rush philosophy.
